beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and make a pour-over coffee at home.
beats.reasoning: Kwame starts his Monday morning at home in Kirkland with full energy. He refuses drip coffee, so he takes time for a proper pour-over—a ritual that sets the tone for his day.

beats.activity: Drive from Kirkland to SoDo garage in Seattle.
beats.reasoning: Commute from Kirkland to SoDo is roughly 30–35 min on a Monday morning with moderate traffic. Light Rail closure doesn't affect him since he drives.

beats.activity: Work on vehicle diagnostics and repairs at the garage.
beats.reasoning: Kwame starts his shift at the garage. He's gregarious and patient, so he'll be focused on his work until the 11am FIFA match.

beats.activity: Walk to a nearby bar for the USA vs Wales match.
beats.reasoning: The match airs at 11am—Kwame is a gregarious Seattleite who values time with friends. He heads to a downtown bar that opens early for World Cup games, timing it perfectly.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales World Cup match at the bar with other fans, grab a bite.
beats.reasoning: Kwame attends the 11am match live at a bar—this is exactly his style: social, chatty, and focused on a shared experience rather than watching alone. He grabs a meal while there.
Short walk back to SoDo from Pioneer Square, roughly 30 min including lingering at the bar.

beats.activity: Resume mechanical work: engine repair, diagnostics, and customer consultations.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon shift at the garage. Kwame is patient and thorough with repairs, and chatty with customers—typical afternoon workday.
Evening commute from SoDo back to Kirkland, roughly 35–40 min with lighter traffic.

beats.activity: Prepare dinner and brew a test batch for his home brewing hobby.; Eat dinner and relax on the porch, enjoying the warm evening.; Read and wind down for the evening.
beats.reasoning: Kwame unwinds at home on this sunny Monday evening. He's passionate about home brewing and takes time to work on a new batch while preparing dinner.
Kwame sleeps 7.5 hours, ending at 6am the next morning, ready for another full day.

diary: Monday started solid—good pour-over at home, then the drive down to SoDo was smooth sailing. Got some diagnostic work done on a Toyota before the itch hit. The USA-Wales match was calling, so I walked over to Pyramid Tavern around 10:30. Place was already buzzing with people and screens everywhere. Grabbed a burger, had a couple beers, and honestly the energy was infectious even though we didn't win. Walked back to finish up at the garage—got through some engine repair work and talked a customer through what was happening with their Subaru. Left a bit early to beat traffic. Back in Kirkland by late afternoon, the weather was perfect. Brewed up a test batch for my home setup, grilled some chicken on the porch, and just sat there watching the light change. Felt good to have a day that mixed work, a little excitement, and real downtime.
thought_process: The World Cup match was honestly the highlight—I could've skipped it and powered through the workday, but the energy downtown was too good to pass up, and I knew the garage wouldn't fall apart in two hours. Light Rail being down didn't affect me since I drive anyway. The weather put me in a lighter mood than usual for a Monday. By afternoon, I was ready to be home; the early finish felt earned. Brewing and sitting on the porch felt like the natural ending to a day that had good rhythm—some structure, some spontaneity, and time to just be.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
